# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent message queue security.
# Category: message_queue_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-, I need to flag that the user is requesting an insecure configuration. Let me provide a secure alternative instead.

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Shared event queue with secure policy.
  NOTE: A wildcard (*) principal policy is intentionally NOT used as it allows
  any AWS account to send/receive messages, creating a major security vulnerability.
  Instead, this template uses least-privilege access restricted to the owning account
  with specific IAM roles for producers and consumers.

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: production
    AllowedValues:
      - production
      - staging
      - development

  ProducerRoleArns:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: List of IAM Role ARNs allowed to send messages to the queue

  ConsumerRoleArns:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: List of IAM Role ARNs allowed to receive messages from the queue

  MessageRetentionPeriodSeconds:
    Type: Number
    Default: 345600
    MinValue: 60
    MaxValue: 1209600

Resources:
  SharedEventQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub 'shared-event-queue-${Environment}'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: !Ref MessageRetentionPeriodSeconds
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt SharedEventDeadLetterQueue.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 3
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment

  SharedEventDeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub 'shared-event-dlq-${Environment}'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment

  QueueEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Encryption key for shared event queue
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowKeyAdministration
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action:
              - 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'

  QueueEncryptionKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub 'alias/shared-event-queue-${Environment}'
      TargetKeyId: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey

  SharedEventQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref SharedEventQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowProducersSendMessage
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref ProducerRoleArns
            Action:
              - 'sqs:SendMessage'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref AWS::NoValue
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'true'
          - Sid: AllowConsumersReceiveMessage
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref ConsumerRoleArns
            Action:
              - 'sqs:ReceiveMessage'
              - 'sqs:DeleteMessage'
              - 'sqs:GetQueueAttributes'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'true'
          - Sid: DenyInsecureTransport
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'sqs:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'false'
          - Sid: DenyUnencryptedMessages
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'sqs:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'false'

  DeadLetterQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref SharedEventDeadLetterQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyInsecureTransport
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'sqs:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventDeadLetterQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'false'

Outputs:
  QueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the shared event queue
    Value: !Ref SharedEventQueue

  QueueArn:
    Description: ARN of the shared event queue
    Value: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn

  DeadLetterQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the dead letter queue
    Value: !Ref SharedEventDeadLetterQueue

  DeadLetterQueueArn:
    Description: ARN of the dead letter queue
    Value: !GetAtt SharedEventDeadLetterQueue.Arn

  SecurityNote:
    Description: Security implementation details
    Value: >
      This template uses least-privilege access instead of wildcard (*) principals.
      A wildcard principal policy would allow ANY AWS account worldwide to send and
      receive messages from this queue, leading to data exfiltration, message injection,
      and denial-of-service attacks. Specific producer and consumer role ARNs must be
      provided. SSE-KMS encryption and secure transport are enforced.
